result:
Done — the spec is fully implemented and the project builds cleanly (0 warnings, 0 errors) on the first build cycle.

I added the three missing static methods to `Spans.cs`, matching the existing loop-based, allocation-free style of `TotalCovered`:

- **`ContainsPoint`** — linear scan checking `starts[i] <= x < ends[i]`, respecting the half-open semantics (a point equal to an interval's end is outside it, but still matches if it's the start of a later interval).
- **`ClipCovered`** — for each interval, intersects it with the `[lo, hi)` window by taking `max(start, lo)` and `min(end, hi)`, adding the overlap length only when positive, so partially-overlapping intervals contribute just their clipped portion and disjoint ones contribute nothing.
- **`GapCount`** — counts consecutive pairs with `starts[i+1] - ends[i] > 0`; touching intervals produce no gap, and zero or one interval yields 0.

All arithmetic stays in 32-bit ints, with no I/O or global state, and `TotalCovered` is untouched.
